pattern: Every pair with a fixed absolute difference k is really just two values that are k apart, so counting how many times each value has already appeared and multiplying by the number of times value+k has appeared turns an O(n^2) pair scan into a single pass with a frequency table.
hints: Instead of checking every pair directly, count how many times each value appears in the array first.
For a fixed value v, every occurrence of v paired with every occurrence of v + k contributes one valid pair (and this avoids double counting if you only ever look forward from v to v + k).
Sum, over every distinct value v present, count(v) * count(v + k); that total is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: smallest allowed k with repeated values
largest allowed k, values at both extremes
duplicates on both sides multiply the pair count rather than adding it
all values identical, so no pair can have a nonzero difference
values that are k apart in both directions must not be double counted

stress.description: random values across the full range at the maximum array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 99)
    return [nums, k]
